message:
feat(fsfs): add native PDF text extraction for indexing and search

Add support for indexing digital PDFs (with selectable text) alongside
regular text files. When fsfs encounters a .pdf file during indexing,
it extracts the text content using pdf-extract and indexes it through
the normal canonicalization/embedding pipeline. Image-only, encrypted,
or corrupt PDFs are gracefully skipped with appropriate log messages.

Changes:
- Add pdf-extract 0.10 as workspace dependency
- Add is_pdf_file() and try_extract_pdf_text() helpers in runtime.rs
- Modify batch indexing path to extract PDF text before binary check
- Modify live ingest (watcher) path with same PDF-aware logic
- PDFs that yield no extractable text are silently skipped

Also includes concurrent work: LLM query expansion (--expand flag).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -92,6 +92,9 @@ toml = "1.0.3"
 # SHA-256 verification for model manifests
 sha2 = "0.10"
 
+# PDF text extraction (digital PDFs with selectable text)
+pdf-extract = "0.10"
+
 # Benchmarking
 criterion = { version = "0.8.2", features = ["html_reports"] }
 proptest = "1.6"
